_note: Topic queue for the Labelwala.store blog writing workflow. Labelwala makes custom FABRIC/GARMENT labels (woven, printed, care, heat-transfer) for clothing brands, garment manufacturers, and uniform buyers. See BUSINESS.md. Orchestrator picks the next 'queued' entry sorted by priority.

topics.title: Woven vs Printed Labels — Which One Your Clothing Brand Should Use
topics.target_keyword: woven vs printed labels for clothing
topics.notes: Take a clear position per use case. Woven for neck/brand labels, printed for care/large info. Cover cost, feel, durability. India MOQ realities. DIFFERENTIATE from existing blog /printed-fabric-label-at-best-price-in-india/ (that one is pricing/sourcing-focused) — this post is the decision framework. Cross-link to it for the pricing deep-dive.

topics.title: What Your Care Label Must Show — Fiber Content & Wash Symbols in India
topics.target_keyword: care label requirements India fiber content
topics.notes: Compliance topic — be accurate. Fiber content %, care symbols, country of origin. High stakes for exporters. No guessing on legal specifics.

topics.title: Label MOQ Explained — How Many to Order When You're a New Brand
topics.target_keyword: clothing label minimum order quantity India
topics.notes: Cover why low MOQ feels safe but costs more per piece. Setup cost re-paid on re-orders. Labelwala MOQ is 100. Real per-piece math at 100/500/1000.

topics.title: Where to Place Labels on a Garment — Neck, Side Seam, Hem
topics.target_keyword: where to sew labels on clothing
topics.notes: Practical placement guide. Neck (brand), side seam (care), hem (flag tags). What looks premium vs cheap. Link to label size guide blog.

topics.title: Satin, Cotton or Polyester — Picking the Right Label Material
topics.target_keyword: fabric label material types satin cotton polyester
topics.notes: Plain language. Each material with one clear use case and one drawback. Satin = soft/premium, cotton = natural/organic brands, polyester = durable/cheap.

topics.title: Heat-Transfer (Tagless) Labels — When to Skip Stitching Entirely
topics.target_keyword: heat transfer tagless labels clothing India
topics.notes: Pros: no itch, no stitching cost, clean look for activewear/innerwear. Cons: durability, equipment. When tagless is right vs woven.

topics.title: 6 Neck Label Mistakes That Make a Brand Look Cheap
topics.target_keyword: neck label design mistakes clothing brand
topics.notes: Specific mistakes with reasons: too much detail woven small, wrong fold, scratchy material at neck, off-brand thread colour. Be direct about what looks bad.

topics.title: School Uniform Labels — Woven Crests, Name Tapes & Care Labels Done Right
topics.target_keyword: school uniform labels name tapes India
topics.notes: For school/uniform procurement. Bulk name tapes, woven crests, wash-proof, lead time before session start. Link to /segments/school-uniform-logo and /name-number-labels. DIFFERENTIATE from existing blog /stop-losing-clothes-in-hostel-laundry/ (that one is the hostel/parent name-label angle) — this post is institutional/bulk procurement. Cross-link to it.

topics.title: Size Tabs and Hem Tags — The Small Labels That Quietly Matter
topics.target_keyword: size tabs hem tags clothing labels
topics.notes: Often an afterthought. Focus on STRATEGY (when/why to use, fold types, when a hem flag adds brand value) — do NOT repeat dimensions, those are covered in /blog/label-size-guide-all-types. Cross-link to that size guide for the mm specs.

topics.title: How to Set Up Artwork for Woven Labels (So It Actually Weaves Clean)
topics.notes: Why fine detail and gradients fail in weave. Vector files, minimum text height, thread colour limits. Helps reduce sampling back-and-forth.
